question_frequency.rationale: The Pair (in this transcript the 'Pair' role appears to be the assistant responding to the user's prompts) asks essentially no didactic questions. Turn 1 is a full solution dump with walk-through; Turn 2 is the user offering a menu of next steps. There are no questions probing the user's understanding at teachable moments.

question_quality.rationale: No substantive choices were posed by the Pair. The only 'choice' surfaced is a passing remark at the end about keying the match dict on closers vs. openers, which is mentioned but not framed as a question for the user to engage with.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no scaffolding — the complete solution, walk-through, complexity analysis, and edge-case enumeration are all delivered up-front in Turn 1. The user has nothing to discover; everything is spoiled immediately.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and the explanations are clear and well-structured, but the conversational flow is one-directional: the Pair lectures, the user (acting as menu-presenter) offers options, the Pair picks and executes. There is no genuine back-and-forth.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
